title: stellaria pubera var. silvatica
intro: Stellaria pubera var. silvatica, also known as woodland chickweed, is a perennial plant in the Caryophyllaceae family, characterized by its small white flowers and hairy leaves. The medicinal properties of woodland chickweed include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. The health benefits of woodland chickweed include its ability to soothe and calm the skin, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome and other digestive problems. Woodland chickweed can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or salve, and is often used in herbal medicine to treat a range of health issues.
